zh.Princenhage, North Brabant, Netherlands: 荷蘭努-布拉班特普林哈
zh.Princenhage, Breda, Noord-Brabant, Nethe: 荷蘭努-布拉班特普林哈
zh.lifeSummary: 當Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken於1455年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特普林哈時，她的父親Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer35歲，母親Cornelia Van Loobroek33歲。她和Mathijs Peter Dirven至少有2個兒子。她去世於1520年在她的家鄉享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken于1455年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特普林哈时，她的父亲Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer35岁，母亲Cornelia Van Loobroek33岁。她和Mathijs Peter Dirven至少有2个儿子。她去世于1520年在她的家乡享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Veeken
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beacon  .
Flemish (mainly Belgian Limburg): genitivized form of   Beek  .

variants.name: Beacon
meanings: English (mainly Kent): either from a Middle English female personal name Bekin, a pet form of Beatrice, or a habitational name from one or other of the places described by, or with names deriving from, Middle English beken ‘beacon, signal’ (Old English bēacun), for example Beacon Hill in Bishopstone (Sussex) or Beacon End in Stanway (Essex).

nameMeanings.name: Anthonia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Antonia  , modelled on the spelling of   Anthony .

variants.name: Antonia
meanings: feminine form of   Anthony  , unaltered since classical times, when it was a common Roman feminine family name.

nameMeanings.name: Bouden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French:
variant of Bodin , a pet form of Bodo, a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the element bod ‘messenger’.
metonymic occupational name for maker or seller of blood sausages, from boudin ‘blood sausage’, or an unflattering nickname for a big-bellied person. Compare Bodine .

variants.name: Bodine
meanings: Altered form of French   Boudin   or   Bodin  .

History: This surname is listed along with its original form Boudin in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors. It is also listed in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. The Huguenot ancestor of the Bodines was Jean Boudin, who arrived in New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) from France in the second half of the 17th century and settled on Staten Island.

variants.name: Bodin
meanings: ‘Win respect’.

variants.name: Boudinot
meanings: French:
from a pet form of the personal name   Boudin   1.
diminutive of   Boudin   2.

historicEvents.description: The courtyard Episcopal palace at Atrecht had witch burnings.
Beginning of the rule of the Austrian Habsburgs.
The Netherlands is inherited by the future Habsburg Emperor and King of Spain Charles V.
The Catholic King Charles I of Spain ruled the Netherlands.

en.lifeSummary: When Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken was born in 1455, in Princenhage, North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer, was 35 and her mother, Cornelia Van Loobroek, was 33. She had at least 2 sons with Mathijs Peter Dirven. She died in 1520, in her hometown, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken est née en 1455, à Princenhage, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Cornelia Van Loobroek, avait 33 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Mathijs Peter Dirven. Elle est décédée en 1520, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken 1455 in Princenhage, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer 35 und ihre Mutter Cornelia Van Loobroek 33. Sie hatte mit Mathijs Peter Dirven mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1520 im Alter von 65 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken è nata il nel 1455 nel Princenhage, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Cornelia Van Loobroek, aveva 33 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Mathijs Peter Dirven. Morì nel 1520, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Anthonia Bouden van der Veekenが1455年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントプリンセンハッジで生まれた時，父Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwerは35歳，母Cornelia Van Loobroekは33歳であった。彼女はMathijs Peter Dirvenとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1520年に故郷で65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken(이)가 1455년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 프린스하지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cornelia Van Loobroek(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 Mathijs Peter Dirven(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1520년 그녀의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken nasceu em 1455, em Princenhage, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Cornelia Van Loobroek, tinha 33 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Mathijs Peter Dirven. Ela faleceu em 1520, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anthonia Bouden van der Veeken родилась в 1455 г. в местности принценадж, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Boudewijn van der Veecken van de Wouwer было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Cornelia Van Loobroek, 33. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Mathijs Peter Dirven. Она умерла в 1520 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 65 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Mathijs Peter Dirven. Murió en 1520, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años.
